description: Hybrid retrieval pipeline specification. Load when working on retrieval, embeddings, search, or ingestion.
pipeline: ingest → chunk → embed → index → hybrid-retrieve → fuse → rerank → generate → validate

chunking.hard_limit: 1000 tokens per chunk — enforced, not advisory.
principles: Chunk boundaries should respect semantic units (paragraphs, sections) not just token counts.
Every chunk carries enough metadata to trace back to its source document and location.
Chunking strategy is an experimental variable — changes must be validated against NDCG@10.

retrieval.description: Vector-only retrieval is insufficient for production. Dispatch queries concurrently to sparse (BM25) and dense (vector) retrievers, fuse with RRF, rerank with cross-encoder.

sparse_layer.purpose: Keyword precision — captures exact terms, identifiers, alphanumeric codes, rare vocabulary.
process: tokenization: deterministic conversion to token IDs with English stop-word filtering
indexing: vocabulary lookup table with TF-style weighting (term frequency relative to corpus)
sparse_layer.limitation: Cannot handle paraphrasing — high-precision anchor, not standalone retriever.

dense_layer.purpose: Semantic mapping — captures meaning via high-dimensional vector space.

optimization.normalization: L2 normalization — rescale vectors to unit Euclidean distance
optimization.similarity: dot-product via numpy (L2 normalization makes it equivalent to cosine, but faster)
optimization.rationale: Pure matrix algebra — near-instantaneous retrieval without vector database overhead.

persistence.loading: indices loaded directly into memory from disk

fusion.purpose: Scale-agnostic merging of BM25 and dense scores.
process: 1. concurrent-retrieval: top-K candidates (K=50) from both indices
2. list-aggregation: collate into list-of-lists maintaining original rank
3. fused-ranking: apply RRF formula to each document ID

reranking.purpose: Joint query-document analysis — captures subtle contextual relevance that bi-encoders miss.

additional_techniques: contextual-retrieval: add document context to chunks before embedding
query-expansion: rephrase user query into multiple variants for broader recall
self-query: extract metadata filters from natural language queries

agentic_rag.best_for: complex queries spanning 3+ files, codebase navigation
toolkit: list: glob pattern matching — use relative paths for token optimization
search: compiled regex across document lines — returns file names and line numbers
read: content extraction with path validation (is_relative_to) to prevent traversal attacks
production_safety: agent_request_limit: hard cap on tool calls per query
timeout: maximum wall-clock time per execution
error_handling: return messages, never raise exceptions

structured_citations.enforcement: Pydantic models enforce citation structure in outputs

decision_framework: 1. answer requires synthesizing from 3+ files? → agentic
2. target response time is milliseconds? → semantic. Can wait 10-15s? → agentic
3. deployment supports subprocess (ripgrep)? If restricted → semantic
4. budget for multiple LLM calls? If constrained → semantic with hybrid retrieval
5. default: start semantic hybrid, upgrade to agentic when complexity demands it

principles: Indices are stored locally and can be version-controlled.
No database server required for corpora under 1M documents.
Challenge the Vector Database Myth: disk-based numpy outperforms dedicated vector DBs in latency for <1M docs.
Transition to managed vector DB only when corpus exceeds 1M or multi-tenant isolation is required.

evaluation.cold_start: Generate synthetic BEIR-style (Query, Relationship, Corpus) pairs from document chunks. Never ship without an evaluation dataset.
experimental_loop: 5. repeat — every change is validated scientifically
